title: Apple turnovers
subtitle: Puff pastry parcels with spiced apple filling
excerpt: Ready-made puff pastry folded around a spiced apple filling, brushed with egg wash, and baked until golden and flaky. Quick to make at home with a shop-bought pastry sheet.
sourceNotes: Apple turnovers using ready-made puff pastry are documented in British domestic cookery from the mid-twentieth century, when commercially produced puff pastry became widely available. The filling follows the traditional British formula of cooking apple, caster sugar, and warm spice.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze unbaked turnovers on a tray, then transfer to a bag. Bake from frozen at 200°C fan for 25–28 minutes.
recipe.batchNotes: Scales easily — one extra pastry sheet gives 6 more turnovers.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Best eaten the day they are made. The pastry softens by the next day.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven, middle shelf — puff pastry needs high heat to rise and crisp

glossaryTerms.definition: A beaten egg (sometimes with a splash of milk) brushed over raw pastry before baking. As it cooks, the proteins and fat brown the surface, giving the pastry a deep golden colour and a light sheen. Brush lightly and avoid the cut edges, where a heavy coating can glue the layers together and prevent the pastry from rising.

content.text: A turnover is simply a folded pastry parcel — one square of puff pastry, a spoonful of filling, folded in half and crimped shut. The only technique that matters is sealing the edges properly: press firmly with a fork all the way around, or the filling leaks and the pastry steams rather than crisps.

attrs.prepNote: medium (about 400 g), peeled, cored, roughly diced

content.text: Put the diced {{bramley-apples}}, {{caster-sugar}}, {{ground-cinnamon}}, and {{lemon-juice}} in a small saucepan. Cook over medium heat for 5–6 minutes, stirring, until the apple is just tender and the liquid has reduced to a thick syrup. Spread on a plate to cool completely.
Heat the oven to 200°C fan. Line a baking tray with baking paper. Unroll the {{puff-pastry}} sheet and cut into 6 equal squares.
Place a spoonful of cooled filling in the centre of each square. Brush the edges with
. Fold the pastry over the filling to form a triangle. Press the edges together firmly with a fork. Place on the lined tray.
Brush the tops with the remaining egg wash. Bake for 18–22 minutes until the pastry is deep golden and crisp. Leave on the tray for 5 minutes before moving. Dust with {{icing-sugar}} if using.
